examples.sentence: 彼は何でも知ったかぶって話をする。
examples.translation: He talks as if he knows everything.
examples.translationEn: He talks as if he knows everything.
examples.romaji: Kare wa nandemo shittaka butte hanashi o suru.

examples.sentence: 彼女は何も知らないふりをしてぶっていたが、本当は全部わかっている。
examples.translation: She was acting like she didn’t know anything, but in reality, she understands it all.
examples.translationEn: She was acting like she didn’t know anything, but in reality, she understands it all.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa nanimo shiranai furi o shite butte ita ga, hontou wa zenbu wakatte iru.

examples.sentence: 彼はいつも偉そうぶって周りに指図している。
examples.translation: He’s always bossing people around as if he’s so important.
examples.translationEn: He’s always bossing people around as if he’s so important.
examples.romaji: Kare wa itsumo erasou butte mawari ni sashizu shite iru.

examples.sentence: 大人ぶっているけど、まだ子供っぽいところがある。
examples.translation: He acts grown-up, but he still has some childish traits.
examples.translationEn: He acts grown-up, but he still has some childish traits.
examples.romaji: Otona butte iru kedo, mada kodomoppoi tokoro ga aru.

examples.sentence: 三年ぶりに彼に会いました。
examples.translation: I met him for the first time in three years.
examples.translationEn: I met him for the first time in three years.
examples.romaji: San-nen buri ni kare ni aimashita.

examples.sentence: 一週間ぶりにジムに行った。
examples.translation: I went to the gym for the first time in a week.
examples.translationEn: I went to the gym for the first time in a week.
examples.romaji: Isshuukan buri ni jimu ni itta.

examples.sentence: 一ヶ月ぶりに彼からの手紙が来た。
examples.translation: I received a letter from him for the first time in a month.
examples.translationEn: I received a letter from him for the first time in a month.
examples.romaji: Ikkagetsu buri ni kare kara no tegami ga kita.

examples.sentence: 五年ぶりに雪が降った。
examples.translation: It snowed for the first time in five years.
examples.translationEn: It snowed for the first time in five years.
examples.romaji: Go-nen buri ni yuki ga futta.

examples.sentence: 彼はあたかも知っているかのように学者ぶる。
examples.translation: He acts like a scholar, as if he knows everything.
examples.translationEn: He acts like a scholar, as if he knows everything.
examples.romaji: Kare wa atakamo shitteiru ka no you ni gakusha buru.

examples.sentence: 女優ぶるのはやめて、自分自身でいろ。
examples.translation: Stop acting like you’re an actress and just be yourself.
examples.translationEn: Stop acting like you’re an actress and just be yourself.
examples.romaji: Joyu buru no wa yamete, jibun jishin de iro.

examples.sentence: 彼女は常にお嬢様ぶっている。
examples.translation: She’s always putting on the airs of a refined lady.
examples.translationEn: She’s always putting on the airs of a refined lady.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa tsune ni ojou-sama butte iru.

examples.sentence: 私がいじめられていると無邪気ぶる彼女を見て、怒りがこみ上げた。
examples.translation: Seeing her act innocent while I was being bullied made my anger surge.
examples.translationEn: Seeing her act innocent while I was being bullied made my anger surge.
examples.romaji: Watashi ga ijimerarete iru to mujaki buru kanojo o mite, ikari ga komiageta.

examples.sentence: 彼とは友達までだ。それ以上の関係ではありません。
examples.translation: With him, it's only friendship—nothing more.
examples.translationEn: With him, it's only friendship—nothing more.
examples.romaji: Kare to wa tomodachi made da. Sore ijou no kankei dewa arimasen.

examples.sentence: 飲み放題は9時までだ。それ以降は追加料金が必要だ。
examples.translation: The all-you-can-drink deal is only until nine. After that, there's an extra charge.
examples.translationEn: The all-you-can-drink deal is only until nine. After that, there's an extra charge.
examples.romaji: Nomihoudai wa kuji made da. Sore ikou wa tsuika ryoukin ga hitsuyou da.

examples.sentence: 私たちの情報は昨日までだ。それ以降のアップデートはありません。
examples.translation: Our information only goes up to yesterday; there are no further updates.
examples.translationEn: Our information only goes up to yesterday; there are no further updates.
examples.romaji: Watashitachi no jouhou wa kinou made da. Sore ikou no appudeeto wa arimasen.

examples.sentence: 彼の知識は中学生レベルまでだ。それ以上は理解できない。
examples.translation: His knowledge is only at a junior-high level. He can't understand anything more advanced.
examples.translationEn: His knowledge is only at a junior-high level. He can't understand anything more advanced.
examples.romaji: Kare no chishiki wa chuugakusei reberu made da. Sore ijou wa rikai dekinai.

examples.sentence: 100万円を負けるもなんでもない。
examples.translation: Losing a million yen is no big deal at all.
examples.translationEn: Losing a million yen is no big deal at all.
examples.romaji: Hyaku man-en o makeru mo nandemonai.

examples.sentence: 彼が社長だもなんでもない。
examples.translation: It doesn't matter one bit that he's the president.
examples.translationEn: It doesn't matter one bit that he's the president.
examples.romaji: Kare ga shachou da mo nandemonai.

examples.sentence: あなたが来ないもなんでもない。
examples.translation: It doesn't matter at all if you don't come.
examples.translationEn: It doesn't matter at all if you don't come.
examples.romaji: Anata ga konai mo nandemonai.

examples.sentence: それが美しいもなんでもない。
examples.translation: It doesn't matter at all whether it's beautiful.
examples.translationEn: It doesn't matter at all whether it's beautiful.
examples.romaji: Sore ga utsukushii mo nandemonai.

examples.sentence: 雨が降るものとして、傘を持って行きました。
examples.translation: Assuming it would rain, I took an umbrella.
examples.translationEn: Assuming it would rain, I took an umbrella.
examples.romaji: Ame ga furu mono to shite, kasa o motte ikimashita.

examples.sentence: 彼が病気なものとして、代わりに私が出席した。
examples.translation: On the assumption that he was sick, I attended in his place.
examples.translationEn: On the assumption that he was sick, I attended in his place.
examples.romaji: Kare ga byouki na mono to shite, kawari ni watashi ga shusseki shita.

examples.sentence: このケーキが美味しいものとして、みんなに分けよう。
examples.translation: Let’s share this cake, assuming it’s delicious.
examples.translationEn: Let’s share this cake, assuming it’s delicious.
examples.romaji: Kono keeki ga oishii mono to shite, minna ni wakeyou.

examples.sentence: 彼女が学生のものとして、学割を適用しました。
examples.translation: Assuming she is a student, I applied the student discount.
examples.translationEn: Assuming she is a student, I applied the student discount.
examples.romaji: Kanojo ga gakusei no mono to shite, gakuwari o tekiyou shimashita.

examples.sentence: 健康を保たんがために、毎日運動をしています。
examples.translation: I exercise daily in order to maintain my health.
examples.translationEn: I exercise daily in order to maintain my health.
examples.romaji: Kenkou o tamotan ga tame ni, mainichi undou o shite imasu.

examples.sentence: 合格せんがために、一生懸命勉強します。
examples.translation: I study hard in order to pass.
examples.translationEn: I study hard in order to pass.
examples.romaji: Goukaku sen ga tame ni, isshoukenmei benkyou shimasu.

examples.sentence: 明日の試験に備えんがため、今夜は早く寝るつもりです。
examples.translation: I plan to go to bed early so I can prepare for tomorrow’s exam.
examples.translationEn: I plan to go to bed early so I can prepare for tomorrow’s exam.
examples.romaji: Ashita no shiken ni sonaen ga tame, kon'ya wa hayaku neru tsumori desu.

examples.sentence: 彼女の笑顔を見んがために、毎日会いに行きます。
examples.translation: I go to see her every day in order to see her smile.
examples.translationEn: I go to see her every day in order to see her smile.
examples.romaji: Kanojo no egao o min ga tame ni, mainichi ai ni ikimasu.

examples.sentence: 彼は何も知らんばかりに、私の質問を無視した。
examples.translation: He ignored my question as if he didn’t know anything.
examples.translationEn: He ignored my question as if he didn’t know anything.
examples.romaji: Kare wa nanimo shir-an bakari ni, watashi no shitsumon o mushi shita.

examples.sentence: その子は泣かんばかりに顔をしかめた。
examples.translation: The child made a face as if he were about to cry.
examples.translationEn: The child made a face as if he were about to cry.
examples.romaji: Sono ko wa nakan bakari ni kao o shikameta.

examples.sentence: 彼女は笑わんばかりに口元を押さえた。
examples.translation: She covered her mouth as though she were about to burst out laughing.
examples.translationEn: She covered her mouth as though she were about to burst out laughing.
examples.romaji: Kanojo wa warawan bakari ni kuchimoto o osaeta.

examples.sentence: 彼は退屈だと言わんばかりに、ため息を何度もついた。
examples.translation: He sighed over and over as if to say he was bored.
examples.translationEn: He sighed over and over as if to say he was bored.
examples.romaji: Kare wa taikutsu da to iwan bakari ni, tameiki o nando mo tsuita.

examples.sentence: 多少遅くても差し支えないです。
examples.translation: It’s no problem if you’re a bit late.
examples.translationEn: It’s no problem if you’re a bit late.
examples.romaji: Tashou osokute mo sashitsukaenai desu.

examples.sentence: 部屋が少し汚くても差し支えない。
examples.translation: It doesn’t matter if the room is slightly messy.
examples.translationEn: It doesn’t matter if the room is slightly messy.
examples.romaji: Heya ga sukoshi kitanakute mo sashitsukaenai.

examples.sentence: 雨が降っても差し支えない。
examples.translation: It’s not an issue even if it rains.
examples.translationEn: It’s not an issue even if it rains.
examples.romaji: Ame ga futte mo sashitsukaenai.

examples.sentence: 彼がここにいることが差し支えないですか？
examples.translation: Is it okay (no problem) for him to be here?
examples.translationEn: Is it okay (no problem) for him to be here?
examples.romaji: Kare ga koko ni iru koto ga sashitsukaenai desu ka?

examples.sentence: 父が病気の折に、私は留学していました。
examples.translation: When my father was ill, I was studying abroad.
examples.translationEn: When my father was ill, I was studying abroad.
examples.romaji: Chichi ga byouki no ori ni, watashi wa ryuugaku shite imashita.

examples.sentence: 日本へ行く折に、お土産を買ってきてください。
examples.translation: When you go to Japan, please buy souvenirs.
examples.translationEn: When you go to Japan, please buy souvenirs.
examples.romaji: Nihon e iku ori ni, omiyage o katte kite kudasai.

examples.sentence: 子供が寝ている折に、静かにしてください。
examples.translation: Please be quiet when the child is sleeping.
examples.translationEn: Please be quiet when the child is sleeping.
examples.romaji: Kodomo ga nete iru ori ni, shizuka ni shite kudasai.

examples.sentence: 彼に会う折に、この手紙を渡してください。
examples.translation: Please give him this letter when you see him.
examples.translationEn: Please give him this letter when you see him.
examples.romaji: Kare ni au ori ni, kono tegami o watashite kudasai.
